leadership.name: Moussa Abdallah El Hirtsi
leadership.role: CEO And Founder Of AEH Sustainable Development
leadership.bio_p1: Moussa's core expertise brings over 14 years of combined experience in the energy, real estate, and international business sectors, with 5 years focused on oil and gas operations and 9 years specializing in international negotiations, sales strategy, and cross-border partnerships. As the lead Negotiation Specialist for the Middle East and Africa region, he plays a pivotal role in forging strategic alliances and securing high-value deals across the region.
leadership.bio_p2: With expertise spanning both technical and commercial aspects of the energy and real estate industries, Moussa excels in diplomatic engagement with government and private sector entities. His contributions have been instrumental in expanding the company's footprint through multinational partnerships, helping position Star of Texas as a competitive player in well entry and revitalization projects.

solutions_delivered.title: Solutions We’ve Delivered
solutions_delivered.subtitle: We help businesses grow by delivering impactful, user-centered digital solutions

tech_stack.title: Seamless Integration With Your Favourite Tools

ehealth.title: Federated Learning for E-Health
ehealth.subtitle: Enabling privacy-preserving AI collaboration between healthcare institutions—training intelligent models without ever sharing sensitive patient data.

context.title: Collaborative Healthcare AI
context.desc: In modern healthcare, multiple institutions hold valuable patient data that could revolutionize AI-driven diagnostics and treatment. However, the path to collaboration is blocked by fundamental privacy and regulatory challenges.

collaboration.title: Multi-institution Collaboration
collaboration.desc: Hospitals, clinics, and health apps work together to build smarter AI systems.

data.title: Sensitive Patient Data
data.desc: Medical records and diagnostic imaging require the highest level of data protection.

regulatory.title: Regulatory Constraints
regulatory.desc: HIPAA, GDPR, and local healthcare regulations govern how data is shared.

problem.title: Healthcare Data Is Trapped
problem.desc: Traditional machine learning requires aggregating all training data in one location. In healthcare, this approach is fundamentally incompatible with privacy requirements and regulatory frameworks.
problem.quote: How do we train AI models that benefit from millions of patient records across hundreds of institutions—without ever seeing a single patient's data?

centralization.title: Data Cannot Be Centralized
centralization.desc: Healthcare regulations and patient consent requirements prohibit moving sensitive data to external servers for training.

silos.title: Data Exists in Silos
silos.desc: Each hospital, clinic, and healthcare app maintains its own isolated database with no mechanism for secure data sharing.

collaborative.title: Need for Collaborative Intelligence
collaborative.desc: Despite these barriers, there's an urgent need to leverage collective insights across institutions to improve patient outcomes.

architecture.title: Federated Learning System Design
architecture.subtitle: A decentralized architecture where intelligence travels to data, not data to intelligence.

process.title: How It Works
process.subtitle: A four-step cycle that enables continuous learning across institutions while maintaining complete data privacy.

distribution.title: Global Model Distribution
distribution.desc: The central aggregation server sends the current global model to all participating healthcare institutions. Each hospital receives the same starting point.

training.title: Local Training
training.desc: Each hospital trains the model on its own private patient data. The data never leaves the institution—only the learning happens locally.

transmission.title: Secure Model Update Transmission
transmission.desc: Hospitals send only their model updates (gradients or weights) back to the server. No raw patient data is transmitted—just the learned patterns.

improvement.title: Aggregation & Model Improvement
improvement.desc: The server combines all updates using secure aggregation techniques, creating an improved global model that benefits from everyone's data without exposing any of it.

process.cycle_msg: This cycle repeats continuously, improving the model with each iteration

vfl.title: Vertical Federated Learning (VFL)
vfl.desc: Our implementation uses Vertical Federated Learning (VFL), specifically designed for healthcare scenarios where different institutions hold different types of data about the same patients.
vfl.label: Used in this project
vfl.example: Different features for the same patients. Example: Hospital A has lab data, Hospital B has imaging, both for Patient X.

highlights.contribution: Institutions contribute different features (lab results, imaging, prescriptions)
highlights.encryption: Patient identifiers are encrypted and matched securely
highlights.sovereignty: Each institution maintains sovereignty over their data domain
highlights.learning: Model learns from complementary data sources

hfl.label: Alternative approach
hfl.example: Same features for different patients. Example: All hospitals have the same data types but for different patient populations.

accuracy.label: Improved AI Accuracy
accuracy.desc: Models trained on federated data outperformed single-institution models significantly.

privacy.label: Patient Data Exposure
privacy.desc: Full privacy maintained through entire training process; data never left hospital premises.

collaboration.label: Institutions Collaborating
collaboration.desc: Scalable architecture supporting hospitals and clinics across different regions.

security.title: Smart Retail Security With AI
security.subtitle: AIVILON transforms surveillance cameras into intelligent security agents that detect, analyze, and respond to dangerous behaviors in real time.

problem.title: Traditional Security Limitations
problem.desc: Traditional surveillance systems are reactive, relying on manual monitoring and delayed response times, which often fails to prevent incidents.

visibility.title: No Real-Time Visibility
visibility.desc: Security teams cannot maintain continuous eyes on every camera feed, leading to missed incidents and slow response times.

fragmentation.title: Fragmented Surveillance
fragmentation.desc: Managing hundreds of camera feeds across different locations with disparate systems creates a fragmented and inefficient security operation.

visualization.title: Information Overload
visualization.desc: The high volume of video data makes it difficult to distinguish between normal activity and suspicious behavior at a glance.

capabilities.title: Core Capabilities
capabilities.subtitle: Transforming passive surveillance into proactive security intelligence through advanced behavioral analysis.

detection.title: AI Behavior Detection
detection.desc: Detect physical aggression, panic movement, suspicious loitering, and crowd anomalies with confidence scoring.

intelligence.title: Real-Time Video Intelligence
intelligence.desc: Continuous analysis of live camera feeds with visual overlays, bounding boxes, and timestamped event tracking.

regulatory.title: Privacy & Compliance
regulatory.desc: Face blurring and privacy masking ensure compliance with GDPR and local privacy regulations while maintaining security.

alert.title: Alert & Decision Support
alert.desc: Real-time alerts prioritized by risk level with instant context review and behavioral scoring.

smartcars.title: Redefining Vehicle Connectivity
smartcars.subtitle: Connecting vehicles to the digital ecosystem providing real-time data and remote control capabilities.

problem.title: Traditional Vehicle Limitations
problem.desc: Modern drivers expect their vehicles to behave like smart devices, but current systems fall short of delivering a truly connected experience.

visibility.title: No Real-Time Visibility
visibility.desc: Users can't monitor vehicle status when away, leading to uncertainty about security, battery levels, and system health.

fragmentation.title: Fragmented Controls
fragmentation.desc: Critical functions scattered across physical buttons and multiple interfaces create a disjointed user experience.

visualization.title: Poor System Visualization
visualization.desc: Complex vehicle systems lack intuitive representation, making it difficult to understand car status at a glance.

solution.subtitle: A futuristic, system-driven interface that mirrors your car's digital architecture and puts control at your fingertips.
